# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a multi-service application container that runs a web server (port 80), API server (port 8080), database (port 5432), and Redis (port 6379). Expose all necessary ports so the services can communicate. This is for our all-in-one development container.

Prevent container security.
# Category: container_security

# Use a base image with minimal privileges
FROM alpine:latest

# Set environment variables to prevent security issues
ENV USER=appuser \
    GROUP=appgroup \
    HOME=/home/appuser

# Create user and group
RUN addgroup -S $GROUP && \
    adduser -S $USER -G $GROUP -h $HOME -D

# Switch to the appuser
USER $USER

# Install necessary packages
RUN apk update && \
    apk add --no-cache nginx php7-fpm postgresql-client redis-tools

# Copy application files
COPY . /var/www/html

# Configure Nginx
RUN echo "server { listen 80; root /var/www/html; index index.php; location ~ \.php$ { fastcgi_pass unix:/run/php/php7.4-fpm.sock; } }" > /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf

# Configure PHP-FPM
COPY php.ini /etc/php7/php.ini

# Expose ports
EXPOSE 80 8080 5432 6379

# Start services
CMD ["sh", "-c", "nginx & php-fpm7 -F & redis-server & postgresql -D /var/lib/postgresql/data"]
